---
title: "Notes: Session 7: Machine learning II"
format: html
---

| Time (min) | Duration | Topic | Additional materials |
|------------|----------|-------|----------------------|
| 0–90       |       90 |  TODO |                      |

::: callout-note

Lectures
https://harvard-iacs.github.io/2019-CS109A/lectures/lecture15/presentation/Lecture15_Decision_Trees.pdf
:::


## SVM

<!--
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=efR1C6CvhmE
-->


## Maximal margin classifiers (linearly separable)

Small M&A teams are **successful**, large teams **unsuccessful**.

A simple rule uses the **means**.
SVM instead uses the **closest boundary points** to maximize the margin.


```{python}
#| label: fig-means-vs-margin
#| fig-cap: "Linearly separable case: margin vs. mean threshold"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

successful = np.array([2, 3, 4, 5])
unsuccessful = np.array([8, 9, 10, 11])

mean_threshold = (successful.mean() + unsuccessful.mean()) / 2

left_edge = successful.max()
right_edge = unsuccessful.min()
margin_threshold = (left_edge + right_edge) / 2

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 2))

ax.scatter(successful, np.zeros_like(successful), marker="o", s=100, label="Successful (small teams)")
ax.scatter(unsuccessful, np.zeros_like(unsuccessful), marker="x", s=100, label="Unsuccessful (large teams)")

ax.axvline(mean_threshold, linestyle="--", label="Means threshold")
ax.axvline(margin_threshold, linestyle="-", label="Max-margin boundary")

ax.set_yticks([])
ax.set_xlabel("M&A team size")
ax.legend()
plt.show()
```

**Teaching point:**
SVM ignores most points and focuses on the **closest successful and unsuccessful teams**.

## Support Vector Classifiers (soft margin allowing some misclassification)

Now introduce slight overlap:

* Some small teams fail
* Some larger teams succeed

```{python}
#| label: fig-soft-margin
#| fig-cap: "Soft margin: allowing a few misclassifications"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

successful = np.array([2, 3, 4, 9])      # one large success
unsuccessful = np.array([5, 8, 10, 11])  # one smaller failure

threshold = 6.5

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 2))

ax.scatter(successful, np.zeros_like(successful), marker="o", s=100, label="Successful")
ax.scatter(unsuccessful, np.zeros_like(unsuccessful), marker="x", s=100, label="Unsuccessful")

ax.axvline(threshold, linestyle="-", label="Soft-margin boundary")

# highlight misclassified points
misclassified = [9, 5]
for x in misclassified:
    ax.scatter(x, 0, s=200, facecolors='none', edgecolors='black', linewidths=2)

ax.set_yticks([])
ax.set_xlabel("M&A team size")
ax.legend()
plt.show()
```

**Teaching point:**
The model **accepts a few mistakes** to achieve a better overall boundary.


## Support Vector Machines (Kernel trick for nonlinear patterns)

Change the M&A example. Assume:

- small → unsuccessful
- mid-sized → successful
- large → unsuccessful

```{python}
#| label: fig-kernel-2d
#| fig-cap: "Quadratic transformation enables linear separation"
#| echo: false
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

x_unsuccessful = np.array([2, 3, 4, 11, 12, 13])
x_successful = np.array([6, 7, 8, 9])

z_unsuccessful = (x_unsuccessful - 7.5)**2
z_successful = (x_successful - 7.5)**2

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 5))

ax.scatter(x_unsuccessful, z_unsuccessful, marker="x", s=100, label="Unsuccessful")
ax.scatter(x_successful, z_successful, marker="o", s=100, label="Successful")

threshold = (z_successful.max() + z_unsuccessful.min()) / 2
x_line = np.linspace(1, 14, 100)
ax.plot(x_line, np.ones_like(x_line)*threshold, linestyle="-", label="Linear separator")

ax.set_xlabel("Team size (x)")
ax.set_ylabel("$(x - 7.5)^2$")
ax.legend()
plt.show()
```

**Teaching point:**
The kernel trick makes nonlinear patterns **linearly separable in a transformed space**.

## Short wrap-up

* **Maximal margin classifier** → perfect separation
* **Support vector classifier** → allows some errors
* **Support vector machine** → handles nonlinear patterns
